mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling, the discount rate on equities drops and risk appetite improves, so S&P 500 returns are positive over the next month. The signal is falsified if rate cuts are not followed by higher 20-day returns than the unconditional average.
failure_condition: Rejected if 20-day forward SPX returns after declines in FEDFUNDS are not above the unconditional mean, or if triggered instances show negative median returns over a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent level signals labor-market deterioration and impending earnings weakness, which lowers equity prices over the next quarter. The effect is strongest when unemployment is accelerating rather than stable.
failure_condition: Rejected if unemployment-surge episodes are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the average 60-day return is not below baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales growth in RSAFS accelerates, it indicates stronger household demand and near-term revenue growth for large-cap firms, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal fails if sales growth does not translate into higher returns during normal growth conditions.
failure_condition: Rejected if positive RSAFS momentum does not correspond to above-average 20-day SPX returns, or if the rank correlation stays below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in INDPRO signals industrial slowdown and tighter profit margins, so equities underperform over the next 20 trading days. The effect should appear after clear production weakness, not small noise in the series.
failure_condition: Rejected if months with INDPRO drops greater than 0.5% are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the average triggered return is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts and retail sales jointly proxy real domestic demand; when HOUST falls while RSAFS also weakens, the combined signal indicates a broad demand slowdown that should depress S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. Either series alone is insufficient for the full effect.
failure_condition: Rejected if the joint decline in HOUST and RSAFS does not produce lower 60-day SPX returns than either single-series condition, or if interaction terms are not significant in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A high FEDFUNDS rate combined with elevated CPIAUCSL signals restrictive real financing conditions and inflation pressure, which compress equity valuations and reduce returns over the next month. The mechanism requires both tight policy and stubborn inflation.
failure_condition: Rejected if periods with FEDFUNDS above 4.5% and rising CPIAUCSL do not show below-average 20-day SPX returns, or if the joint signal adds no value beyond either proxy alone.

mechanism_narrative: Stable or improving PAYEMS indicates continued payroll expansion and supports corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next week. The signal is weakest when payroll gains are already fully priced and strengthens after a negative labor surprise.
failure_condition: Rejected if PAYEMS improvements are not followed by positive 5-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the short-horizon effect disappears in rolling out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO weakens while UNRATE rises, the joint pattern identifies a cyclical output gap and rising recession risk, which should drive negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The signal is specific to synchronized real-activity deterioration.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.2pp and INDPRO falls month-over-month
failure_condition: Rejected if synchronized weakening in INDPRO and rising UNRATE fails to predict negative 60-day SPX returns, or if triggered episodes have positive median returns over any 3-year window.
